hints.factum: 'factum' is a perfect passive participle — it combines with 'est' to form the perfect passive

steps.prompt: What verb do 'factum est' together form?

hints.Present of 'esse' (is made): 'factum est' = participle + 'est' forms the perfect passive, not present
hints.Imperfect of 'facere' (was making): Imperfect active would be 'faciebat' — 'factum est' is perfect passive
hints.Future of 'fieri' (will become): Future of 'fieri' would be 'fiet' — 'factum est' is past tense

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the principal parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fingo, fingere, finxi, fictum: These are the principal parts of 'fingere' (to shape/mold)

hints.Accusative: 'Verbum' neuter nom. and acc. are identical, but it's the subject of 'factum est'

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'Verbum' with -um ending is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — '-um' is characteristic of 2nd declension neuter
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'Verbum' is 2nd declension

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'caro' with genitive 'carnis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'caro' with stem 'carn-' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'caro' with genitive 'carnis' is 3rd declension

hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns in a case — 'Et' joins clauses
hints.Adverb: 'Et' can sometimes mean 'also', but here it connects this sentence to the preceding verse
hints.Interjection: Interjections are exclamations — 'Et' is a coordinating conjunction

translation: And the Word was made flesh
